channel.title: Dotnet on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Dotnet on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: dotnet: NuGet Package Pruning: Cleaner Dependencies and Actionable Vulnerability Reports
item.description:
In .NET 10, package pruning removes platform-provided packages from the dependency graph. With transitive auditing enabled by default, projects using the new defaults see about 70% fewer transitive vulnerability reports than with the previous defaults.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Process API Improvements in .NET 11
item.description:
The post describes Process API changes in .NET 11, adding higher-level process-starting APIs, safer output capture to avoid deadlocks, control over handle inheritance, lifetime management features, and a lighter API surface based on SafeProcessHandle.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: .NET MAUI Moves to CoreCLR in .NET 11
item.description:
In .NET 11, .NET MAUI apps on Android, iOS, and Mac Catalyst run on CoreCLR by default. This aligns MAUI with the same runtime used across the rest of .NET.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: .NET 11 Preview 4 is now available!
item.description:
The .NET 11 Preview 4 blog post announces the preview release and notes updates across the runtime, SDK, libraries, ASP.NET Core, .NET MAUI, C#, Entity Framework Core, and related areas. The provided excerpt does not include the specific feature or bug-fix details.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: .NET and .NET Framework May 2026 servicing releases updates
item.description:
Microsoft published a May 2026 recap of servicing updates for .NET and .NET Framework. The provided text does not include specific version numbers, fixes, or security details.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Copilot Studio gets faster with .NET 10 on WebAssembly
item.description:
Microsoft Copilot Studio upgraded its WebAssembly engine to .NET 10. The migration was described as straightforward, simplified deployment, and improved performance for end users.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Durable Workflows in the Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description:
Microsoft Agent Framework now supports durable AI agent workflows using the Durable Task runtime. The post shows a progression from in-process console apps to parallel agent execution and Azure Functions hosting for serverless runs.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Microsoft Agent Framework – Building Blocks for AI Part 3
item.description:
Part 3 of the Microsoft Agent Framework series shows how to build .NET AI agents using tools, multi-turn conversations, memory, and graph-based workflows. It ties together the earlier parts into the framework’s main building blocks for agent development.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Building an AI-Powered Conference App with .NET’s Composable AI Stack
item.description:
The post describes building ConferencePulse, an AI-powered conference assistant, using .NET’s composable AI stack. It highlights Microsoft.Extensions.AI, DataIngestion, VectorData, Model Context Protocol, and Microsoft Agent Framework as the main components.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Governing MCP tool calls in .NET with the Agent Governance Toolkit
item.description:
The post describes how to govern MCP tool calls in .NET using the Agent Governance Toolkit. It focuses on policy-based access control, security scanning, and response sanitization to reduce risk in AI agent systems.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: VSTest is Removing its Newtonsoft.Json Dependency
item.description:
VSTest will remove its Newtonsoft.Json dependency in .NET 11 and Visual Studio 18.8. The post says it explains who is affected and how to adjust to the change.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Welcome to SkiaSharp 4.0 Preview 1
item.description:
SkiaSharp 4.0 Preview 1 has been released. The post also notes that Uno Platform has joined as a co-maintainer.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: High-Performance Distributed Caching with .NET and Postgres on Azure
item.description:
The post introduces a .NET walkthrough for adding distributed caching with Postgres on Azure to improve performance, reduce latency, and lower load on downstream systems. It presents current caching best practices and packages them into a reusable application example.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Combining API versioning with OpenAPI in .NET 10 applications
item.description:
The post explains how to add API versioning to .NET 10 applications and document those versions with OpenAPI. It focuses on using both together for clearer API management and documentation.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: What’s new for .NET in Ubuntu 26.04
item.description:
Ubuntu 26.04 ships with .NET 10. The post explains how to install .NET 10 from the Ubuntu archive, use the new -resolute container tags, build Native AOT apps, and install .NET 8 and 9 from the dotnet-backports PPA.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: .NET 10.0.7 Out-of-Band Security Update
item.description:
Microsoft released .NET 10.0.7 as an out-of-band security update to address CVE-2026-40372. The update is security-focused and published outside the regular release cycle.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Writing Node.js addons with .NET Native AOT
item.description:
Microsoft describes replacing a C++ Node.js addon with a C# implementation built with .NET Native AOT, removing the Python build dependency. The post explains how to create a Node.js native addon in C# using N-API, LibraryImport, and UnmanagedCallersOnly.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Pin Clustering in .NET MAUI Maps
item.description:
The .NET MAUI Map control in .NET MAUI 11 now supports pin clustering, automatically grouping nearby pins into cluster markers. The post also covers configuring separate clustering groups and handling cluster tap events on Android and iOS.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: .NET and .NET Framework April 2026 servicing releases updates
item.description:
The post is a brief recap of April 2026 servicing updates for .NET and .NET Framework. It does not include specific version details or change items in the provided text.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: .NET 11 Preview 3 is now available!
item.description:
Microsoft announced .NET 11 Preview 3, covering updates across the runtime, SDK, libraries, ASP.NET Core, .NET MAUI, C#, Entity Framework Core, and container images. The provided excerpt does not include specific feature details.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Your Migration’s Source of Truth: The Modernization Assessment
item.description:
The post introduces GitHub Copilot Modernization and focuses on the assessment report as the source of truth for cloud modernization work. It frames the report as part of the migration process and positions it as the primary reference for modernization planning.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: ASP.NET Core 2.3 end of support announcement
item.description:
ASP.NET Core 2.3 will reach end of support on April 7, 2027. Microsoft says users should plan to upgrade applications to a currently supported .NET version.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Explore union types in C# 15
item.description:
C# 15 adds preview support for union types, letting you define a closed set of case types with implicit conversions and exhaustive pattern matching. The post also notes this fits into a broader exhaustiveness roadmap.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Generative AI for Beginners .NET: Version 2 on .NET 10
item.description:
Microsoft announced Version 2 of Generative AI for Beginners .NET, a free course rebuilt for .NET 10. It adds Microsoft.Extensions.AI, updated RAG patterns, and new agent framework content across five lessons focused on production-ready AI apps.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Ten Months with Copilot Coding Agent in dotnet/runtime
item.description:
The .NET team reports on ten months of using GitHub Copilot Coding Agent in dotnet/runtime and summarizes lessons from cloud-AI-assisted development. The post is a data-driven retrospective rather than a feature release.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Accelerating .NET MAUI Development with AI Agents
item.description:
Microsoft says custom-built AI agents are being used to improve the .NET MAUI contribution workflow, with reported 50–70% reductions in issue resolution time. The post also claims increased test coverage and code quality.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: RT.Assistant: A Multi-Agent Voice Bot Using .NET and OpenAI
item.description:
The post describes RT.Assistant, a real-time multi-agent voice bot built with .NET, F#, Microsoft.Extensions.AI, .NET MAUI, and the OpenAI Realtime API. It is presented as a guest blog post on the .NET Blog.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Modernize .NET Anywhere with GitHub Copilot
item.description:
Microsoft introduced the modernize-dotnet agent to help assess apps, generate upgrade plans, and modernize .NET projects. It is available in Visual Studio, VS Code, GitHub Copilot CLI, and GitHub.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: .NET 10.0.5 Out-of-Band Release – macOS Debugger Fix
item.description:
Microsoft released .NET 10.0.5 as an out-of-band update to fix a critical debugger crash on macOS when using Visual Studio Code.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: .NET 11 Preview 2 is now available!
item.description:
Microsoft announced .NET 11 Preview 2, with updates across the runtime, SDK, libraries, ASP.NET Core, Blazor, C#, and .NET MAUI. The post points readers to the preview release details, but the provided excerpt does not list specific changes.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: .NET and .NET Framework March 2026 servicing releases updates
item.description:
This post recaps the March 2026 servicing updates for .NET and .NET Framework. It is a brief announcement and does not include technical details in the provided text.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Extend your coding agent with .NET Skills
item.description:
Microsoft introduced the dotnet/skills repository, describing a set of .NET “agent skills” intended to extend coding agents and improve agent-driven development workflows.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Release v1.0 of the official MCP C# SDK
item.description:
MCP C# SDK v1.0 focuses on improved authorization handling, expanded metadata support, and added patterns for tool calling and long-running operations.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Vector Data in .NET – Building Blocks for AI Part 2
item.description:
Microsoft describes Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData as a .NET library that standardizes access to vector databases for semantic search scenarios.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: The Dongle Died at Midnight – WinForms Agent Saved my German Mom’s Business Trip
item.description:
A .NET Blog post describing how the author used a WinForms agent/AI-assisted development workflow to quickly build a replacement time-study app after a hardware dongle failed during a business trip.

  • Source: .NET
